title: dryopteris subtriphylla
intro: Dryopteris subtriphylla, also known as the autumn fern or autumn lady fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The health benefits of Dryopteris subtriphylla include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this fern are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
